key_insight: The paper shows that LLM agents need an internal, capability-first world model to do grounded "what-if" planning, not just to imitate look-ahead text.
engineering_relevance: For agentic systems, the training pipeline matters: predictive competence must be built before formatting and reinforcement if you want reliable long-horizon decisions.
method: WM-AMT: mid-train the policy to develop latent predictive/world-model ability.
FE-SFT: fine-tune the model to express rollout-and-value predictions in a consistent text format.
FC-RL: reinforce calibrated, useful foresight for better planning quality.
evidence_signal: The approach is evaluated on search and mathematical reasoning tasks, where it consistently outperforms other training baselines.
watch_item: A key implementation risk is the format-capability gap: supervised look-ahead traces can produce superficial foresight unless the model first learns genuine predictive grounding.
paper_title: Internalizing the Future: A Unified Agentic Training Paradigm for World Model Planning
